policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released final climate risk management guidelines mandating banks with assets over ₹1 lakh crore to conduct climate stress tests by March 2027, with PD/LGD adjustments for physical risk hotspots. Banks must establish dedicated climate risk units and report portfolio-level transition risk metrics quarterly.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 and Climate Scenario Analysis
policy.description: SEBI amended BRSR Core requirements effective FY2027, mandating top 500 listed companies to disclose Scope 3 emissions and climate scenario analysis aligned with ISSB S2. Assurance requirements now extend to climate-related financial disclosures.

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Framework for Index-Based Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft guidelines enabling insurers to offer parametric products triggered by rainfall indices, temperature thresholds, and cyclone wind speeds. The framework permits banks to distribute parametric covers as add-ons to agricultural and MSME lending products.

policy.title: BCBS Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Disclosure Requirements for Banks
policy.description: Basel Committee issued final Pillar 3 climate disclosure standards requiring internationally active banks to report climate-adjusted credit risk metrics and concentration exposures to high-emission sectors by January 2028. Physical risk disclosures must include geographic granularity at district/regional level.

policy.title: EU Adopts Delegated Acts for CSRD Sector-Specific Standards Including Financial Institutions
policy.description: European Commission adopted sector-specific ESRS for banks and insurers under CSRD, requiring granular financed emissions reporting and climate transition plan disclosures by 2027. Standards include specific metrics for exposure to physical climate risks in loan portfolios.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated climate scenarios incorporating revised IPCC AR7 physical risk projections with higher-resolution regional damage functions for South Asia. Scenarios now include acute event frequency multipliers relevant for parametric product calibration.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial for the bank's corporate lending portfolio across India. The engagement includes scenario-based PD adjustments for flood and cyclone exposure at asset level.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Risk Scores for Bank Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's released Climate on Demand India module covering 650 districts with flood, heat stress, and water scarcity scores mapped to PIN code level. Product integrates with Moody's CreditView for direct PD overlay calculations.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B for ESG SaaS Expansion to Financial Services
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed Series B led by Accel and Bertelsmann India to expand its ESG management platform into banking and NBFC sectors. Funds will support development of BRSR-aligned climate risk modules for portfolio companies.

competitors.title: XDI Releases APAC Banking Edition with India State-Level Risk Benchmarks
competitors.description: XDI launched banking-focused physical risk analytics covering 12 APAC markets including India with state and district-level asset risk benchmarks. Product provides pre-built integrations for Basel Pillar 3 climate disclosure reporting.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Yes Bank for Financed Emissions Measurement
competitors.description: Watershed announced partnership with Yes Bank to deploy its enterprise carbon platform for Scope 3 Category 15 financed emissions tracking across the bank's corporate loan book. Implementation targets PCAF-aligned reporting by Q4 2026.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Launches Natural Capital Risk Tool for Indian Lenders
competitors.description: GIST Advisory released a natural capital and biodiversity risk screening tool specifically designed for Indian banks assessing agriculture and infrastructure lending portfolios. Tool maps TNFD-aligned nature dependencies to loan exposures.

research.title: Climate Risk and Credit: A Framework for Integrating Physical Hazards into PD Models
research.description: IFC framework presents methodology for adjusting probability of default using physical hazard exposure data, showing agricultural portfolios in South Asia face 15-40% PD uplift under RCP 4.5 by 2035. Report provides calibration templates for flood, drought, and heat stress perils.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Scaling Solutions in Emerging Markets
research.description: Swiss Re analysis finds parametric products could close 60% of the $25B South Asian climate protection gap, with bank-distributed agricultural covers showing 3x faster claims settlement. Report identifies rainfall deficit and excess rainfall as highest-potential triggers for India.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Challenges and Opportunities for Financial Institutions
research.description: World Bank study quantifies ESG data coverage at only 23% for listed companies in South Asia versus 78% in OECD markets, with physical risk and Scope 3 data showing largest gaps. Report recommends alternative data approaches including satellite imagery and supply chain proxies.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banks: Methodology and Initial Results
research.description: CRISIL pilot study across 8 public sector banks finds 18% of agricultural loan exposure concentrated in severe flood-risk districts, with potential NPAs increasing 220 bps under extreme monsoon scenarios. Study develops district-level hazard-to-credit-risk mapping framework.

research.title: Climate Scenario Analysis for Banking Supervision: Lessons from Early Adopters
research.description: BIS paper reviews climate stress testing exercises across 15 jurisdictions finding median physical risk capital impact of 8-12% for regionally concentrated portfolios. Report highlights data granularity and hazard model selection as key methodological challenges for emerging market supervisors.

research.title: Advancing Climate Risk Transfer in South Asia: The Role of Index-Based Solutions
research.description: UNEPFI analysis of 45 parametric pilots in South Asia finds basis risk averages 12-18% for rainfall products but drops below 8% with district-level indices. Report recommends embedding parametric covers within bank lending products to achieve distribution scale.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create an immediate compliance market, but Jupiter and Moody's are moving fast to capture it—StepChange must accelerate bank pilots now.
body: The dominant signal across today's intelligence is unmistakable: Indian financial sector climate risk regulation has crossed from consultation to mandate. RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's BRSR expansion, and IRDAI's parametric framework collectively create a compliance-driven demand surge that will peak over the next 18 months. Global players are already positioning to capture this market, and the window for domestic climate intelligence providers to establish incumbency is narrowing rapidly.

The RBI guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development for StepChange's core business. The requirement for banks above ₹1 lakh crore to conduct climate stress tests with PD/LGD adjustments by March 2027 validates our product thesis entirely—but also sets an aggressive timeline. Critically, the mandate for 'physical risk hotspot' identification at portfolio level means banks need exactly the district-level hazard-to-credit mapping that CRISIL's pilot (research-4) demonstrates. StepChange should immediately package its physical risk intelligence as 'RBI Climate Compliance Modules' and target the 25-30 banks that fall under this threshold.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and Moody's India-specific launch (comp-2) represent direct competitive threats that validate market timing but challenge our positioning. Jupiter brings global credibility and HDFC brings proof of enterprise readiness to spend. However, both are adapting global models to India rather than building India-first. StepChange's advantage lies in granular local hazard data and regulatory context—we must lean into CRISIL-style district-level methodologies and position explicitly against 'imported models with Indian wrappers.' The Sprih fundraise (comp-3) signals VC confidence in Indian ESG software for FIs, suggesting partnership or positioning against them as they move downstream into climate.

The IFC framework (research-1) and Swiss Re parametric study (research-2) provide immediate commercial ammunition. IFC's 15-40% PD uplift figures for South Asian agriculture should be referenced in every bank conversation—these are credible third-party numbers that justify climate risk pricing. Swiss Re's finding that bank-distributed parametric covers achieve 3x faster claims settlement directly supports our parametric insurance bet and should inform product development conversations with IRDAI-ready insurers. The World Bank data gaps study (research-3) reinforces our global south data thesis: at 23% ESG coverage, there's a structural opportunity for alternative data providers.

Watch for: RBI's selection of pilot banks for supervised climate stress tests, likely announced Q3 2026—being a data provider to those pilots would be transformative. Action item: Schedule meetings with compliance heads at the top 10 PSU banks within 30 days, leading with the March 2027 deadline and IFC's PD uplift methodology as the technical anchor.
